methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company that has raised €100M to date, founded by Julien Dereux. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 companies across the nuclear energy sector: 3 public entities (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The nuclear energy sector contains a limited number of comparables at Calogena's stage and valuation tier. Filtering for same-stage peers yielded only 1 company; adjacent-stage peers numbered 1; and no peers fell within similar valuation tiers. This subset of 2 datapoints falls short of the minimum 3 required for a reliable median calculation. Broadening the filter to all 8 nuclear energy private companies would introduce stage and maturity heterogeneity that would reduce analytical precision rather than enhance it.

Given the constraints on peer-median analysis, alternative valuation methods would be required to triangulate a defensible range—such as discounted cash flow analysis, revenue multiples applied to early projections, or staged venture capital method frameworks. Without those inputs in the provided data, a specific valuation range cannot be derived. Key uncertainties include the timing and quantum of regulatory approval, commercialization risk inherent in frontier nuclear technologies, and the heterogeneity of business models across the peer set.
